message:
⚡ Lazy-load ECharts in 18 card files via shared LazyEChart wrapper (#10151)

Replace direct `import ReactECharts from 'echarts-for-react'` in 18
card/chart components with a shared `LazyEChart` wrapper that uses
React.lazy() + Suspense to defer the ~1.14 MB ECharts vendor chunk
from the critical loading path.

The 6 shared chart components in components/charts/ keep their direct
imports since they are already behind route-level lazy loading.

Signed-off-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>
Co-authored-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+import { lazy, Suspense, type Ref } from 'react'
+import type { EChartsReactProps } from 'echarts-for-react/lib/types'
+import type EChartsReact from 'echarts-for-react'
+
+const SKELETON_MIN_HEIGHT_PX = 120
+
+const ReactEChartsLazy = lazy(() => import('echarts-for-react'))
+
+interface LazyEChartProps extends EChartsReactProps {
+  ref?: Ref<EChartsReact>
+}
+
+function ChartSkeleton({ style }: { style?: React.CSSProperties }) {
+  return (
+    <div
+      className="animate-pulse bg-muted/30 rounded w-full"
+      style={{ minHeight: SKELETON_MIN_HEIGHT_PX, ...style }}
+    />
+  )
+}
+
+export function LazyEChart({ ref, ...props }: LazyEChartProps) {
+  return (
+    <Suspense fallback={<ChartSkeleton style={props.style} />}>
+      <ReactEChartsLazy ref={ref} {...props} />
+    </Suspense>
+  )
+}
